summary: Broad item decompilation sweep replacing many item placeholder stubs with matching C, tightening item headers and item-var/attribute layouts, and typing several formerly unknown prototypes. The decomp.dev bot reported +8716 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, and 51 new matches; the PR body was empty and no human review comments were captured.

key_files.role: Expanded character-item structs, including Climbers Ice attributes, Arwing laser Vec3 fields, Gamewatch parachute attributes, and Hinoarashi item vars/attributes.
Expanded common-item attributes/item vars for Flipper, Tincle, ZGShell, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, and White Bea.
Added the hinoarashi item-var union member to Item.
Matched Flipper angle update, motion 3 physics/collision, state reset, and clank/hit-shield handlers.
Matched Tools spawn/setup, landing transition, collision, clank, hit-shield, and absorbed handlers.
Matched multiple White Bea behavior transitions and physics functions.
Matched Hinoarashi setup, accessory/motion, damage-received, and effect-spawn behavior.
Matched Arwing laser cleanup, motion animation helpers, and a bone-position effect/audio helper.
Matched Game & Watch Chef item spawn helper with parent/debug/link setup.
Matched Game & Watch Parachute spawn helper and motion-1 animation removal/flag logic.
Matched Mewtwo/Kirby Shadow Ball destroyed cleanup and shield-bounce velocity-angle update.
Matched Yoshi egg throw spawn helper and effect/audio initialization.
Matched Super Scope charge-cost lookup and adjusted velocity reset helper usage.
Matched ZGShell initialization and clanked response.

what_changed: Replaced numerous placeholder markers with concrete matching implementations across item modules, including Flipper, Tools, White Bea, Hinoarashi, Gamewatch Chef/Parachute, Yoshi Egg Throw, Arwing Laser, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Klap, Patapata, SScope, Tincle, and ZGShell.

Expanded item attribute and item-var structs instead of leaving opaque padding, notably itFlipper_DatAttrs, itTincle_ItemVars, itTincleAttributes, itWhiteBeaAttributes, itHinoarashi_ItemVars, itHinoarashiAttributes, itGamewatchparachuteAttributes, and itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars.
Promoted Arwing laser item vars at xE18/xE24 from six s32 slots to two Vec3 fields, enabling code that copies bone/world positions and stores previous positions.
Changed Mewtwo Shadow Ball item vars so x2C is an HSD_GObj* followed by a smaller float array, matching owner cleanup logic in it_2725_Logic101_Destroyed.
Added the hinoarashi member to the Item item-var union, making newly added Hinoarashi code access typed item variables instead of anonymous storage.

Typed several headers that previously used UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, including it_802C1854, it_80290C38, it_80290CE8, it_8028EB88, it_802E1C84, it_8029A31C, it_80291D38, it_802EBD14, it_802EEFA8, and it_802EF098.
Implemented common SpawnItem initialization patterns for item creation helpers such as it_802C837C, it_802C6C38, it_802B2A10, and it_802EEFA8.
Added effect, audio, and owner-link behavior where needed, using efSync_Spawn, efAsync_Spawn, efLib_DestroyAll, Item_8026AE84, db_80225DD8, Item_8026AB54, and fighter callbacks.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increasing to 58.97% (+0.22%) and matched data to 37.69% (+48 bytes), with 51 new matches.
smart_moves: Struct/header work was done alongside function decompilation, so new C could use typed fields like attrs->x24, attrs->xE, hinoarashi.x60.b0, and mewtwoshadowball.x2C instead of brittle casts or raw padding.

Spawn helpers followed the recurring Item_80268B18 pattern: fill SpawnItem, zero velocities, set parent fields, set x44_flag.b0, then run item-specific parent/link setup only after a non-NULL gobj is returned.

Owner cleanup was carefully preserved for linked projectiles: Climbers string clears owner and linked item gobjs, Arwing laser clears neighbor backreferences, and Mewtwo Shadow Ball calls the appropriate Mewtwo/Kirby cleanup callback before nulling x2C.

Matching-only scaffolding was used where evidence required it, including PAD_STACK calls, an unused local array in it_802C2750, a static outline wrapper for it_80291D38, and #pragma dont_inline around duplicate Flipper clank/shield handlers.

The diff reused existing engine helpers such as itResetVelocity, itColl_BounceOffVictim, lbVector_Mirror, lb_8000B1CC, mpCollSetFacingDir, and item collision/animation helpers rather than open-coding all behavior.
Duplicate event-handler behavior was intentionally kept duplicated for matching in Flipper and Tools instead of prematurely abstracting it into shared helpers that might change codegen.
decomp_lessons: For item work, matching C often depends on first recovering the item-specific attribute layout; partial padding splits are useful when only some offsets are known.
Do not leave UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once callsites and return values are understood; typed prototypes can unlock downstream matches and reduce unsafe assumptions.
When item vars contain vectors or object pointers, promote them from integer/float blobs as soon as code evidence shows Vec3 assignment or HSD_GObj ownership semantics.
Projectile/item spawn helpers in this codebase have a recognizable initialization order; future work should copy the established SpawnItem field order and post-spawn parent/link calls.
PAD_STACK, local dummy variables, outline wrappers, and dont_inline pragmas remain legitimate matching tools when the generated stack frame or call shape is otherwise wrong.
Repeated clank, hit-shield, and absorbed handlers may need near-identical bodies with different stack padding to match; avoid deduplicating before confirming codegen.
Effect and audio side effects are part of the match, not just gameplay polish: numeric effect IDs and Item_8026AE84 calls were included in several matched item functions.
When no PR body or review comments exist, rely on diff and decomp.dev metrics only; do not infer reviewer intent beyond the merge and bot report.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retain address-style names like it_80290C38, it_802EEFA8, and it_2725_Logic22_Absorbed until semantics are clearer.
Motion callbacks use the established pattern it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Anim, it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Phys, and it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Coll when the item and motion slot are known.
Item event callbacks use LogicNN plus event names such as Logic20_Clanked, Logic20_HitShield, Logic101_Destroyed, and Logic27_DmgReceived.
Struct fields with unknown semantics keep offset-based names such as x4, xC, xE18, xE1C_b0, and pad_08 while preserving offset comments.
Item-specific types use module prefixes such as itFlipper_DatAttrs, itWhiteBeaAttributes, itHinoarashi_ItemVars, itGamewatchparachuteAttributes, and itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars.
Headers retain address comments before prototypes, for example /* 290C38 */ void it_80290C38(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, f32);.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with several sizes, including 4, 8, 12, 16, and 24 bytes, to preserve stack layout in newly matched functions.
Used #pragma dont_inline on/reset around it_3F14_Logic20_Clanked and it_3F14_Logic20_HitShield to keep Flipper handlers matching.
Added a static s32 it_80291D38_outline wrapper so it_80291FA8 calls the cost helper through the expected outline shape.
Added a static inline itTools_UnkMotion9_Coll_inline helper for the landing transition while keeping the collision function's generated code shape.

Kept exact float constants and comparisons visible, including 30.0f animation-frame checks, 0.17453292f random angle spread, 0.2f White Bea bounce scaling, 10.0f Tincle divisor, and M_TAU angle wrapping.
Used explicit effect/audio IDs from the diff, such as efSync_Spawn(0x40E), efSync_Spawn(arg1 + 0x44E), efAsync_Spawn 0x4CE/0x4CF, Item_8026AE84 0x2734, and Item_8026AE84 0x44618.
Matched pointer/owner cleanup by checking NULLs and exact ownership relationships before clearing fields.
Used HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi with ABS-derived ranges to preserve random behavior and codegen in Klap, White Bea, and ZGShell.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so rationale is inferred only from the title, diff, and decomp.dev report.
decomp.dev reported 51 new matches, +8716 matched code bytes, and +48 matched data bytes for GALE01.
The bot report listed some stage-unit match changes as well as item-unit matches, but the changed files in this PR slice are all under src/melee/it.
